findings.issue: The Docker build CI step can pass even when `docker compose build` fails because the build command is piped through `tee` without `pipefail`; GitHub's default `bash -e` does not fail on a non-final pipeline command.
findings.citation: .github/workflows/remote-hive-build.yml:73 — `docker compose --env-file .env.remote build --no-cache remote-server 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ci-build.log`; command proof: `bash -e -c 'false 2>&1 | tee /dev/null >/dev/null; echo survived-without-pipefail'; printf 'exit=%s\n' $?` output `survived-without-pipefail` and `exit=0`; with `bash -e -o pipefail` output `exit=1`.
findings.remediation:
Add pipefail before the build pipeline:
```yaml
      - name: Build remote-server image
        run: |
          set -o pipefail
          export PNPM_VERSION=$(jq -r .packageManager package.json | sed 's/pnpm@//')
          cp crates/remote/.env.remote.dev crates/remote/.env.remote
          echo 'LOOPS_EMAIL_API_KEY=ci-placeholder' >> crates/remote/.env.remote
          (cd crates/remote && docker compose --env-file .env.remote build --no-cache remote-server 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ci-build.log)
```

findings.issue: The remote Dockerfile does not pin a default pnpm version, so direct `docker build -f crates/remote/Dockerfile .` invocations expand the Corepack target to `pnpm@` unless Compose supplies `PNPM_VERSION`.
findings.citation: crates/remote/Dockerfile:9-11 — `ARG PNPM_VERSION` has no default and line 11 runs `corepack prepare "pnpm@${PNPM_VERSION}" --activate`; command proof: `PNPM_VERSION=; printf 'expanded corepack argument: pnpm@%s\n' "$PNPM_VERSION"` output `expanded corepack argument: pnpm@`.
findings.remediation:
Pin the Dockerfile ARG default to the packageManager version:
```dockerfile
ARG PNPM_VERSION=10.25.0
RUN corepack enable \
 && corepack prepare "pnpm@${PNPM_VERSION}" --activate
```
